title: Zara and the Shy Bunny
synopsis: When Zara discovers a scared little bunny hiding in her garden, she learns that patience and kindness can help make a wonderful new friend.

chapters.title: A New Friend in the Garden

pages.text: Zara loved playing in her backyard garden. She had beautiful brown skin that glowed in the sunshine, and her black curly hair bounced as she ran between the colorful flowers. Today, she was looking for the perfect spot to have a picnic with her teddy bear when she heard a soft rustling sound behind the big sunflowers.

illustration.description: A cheerful young Black girl with curly hair in two puffs, wearing a bright yellow sundress, kneeling in a vibrant garden. Tall sunflowers tower behind her, with red roses and purple petunias scattered around. Her teddy bear sits on a small checkered picnic blanket nearby. The garden has a textured, construction paper-like background with bold primary colors.
illustration.alt: Zara, a young Black girl, exploring her colorful garden with her teddy bear

pages.text: Zara felt curious but also a little scared. "Who's there?" she called softly. A tiny gray bunny peeked out from behind a sunflower, its little nose twitching nervously. The bunny looked scared too, with big worried eyes. Zara's heart felt sad seeing the frightened little creature. She wanted to help, but she didn't want to make the bunny more scared.

illustration.description: Split scene showing Zara crouching down with a gentle, concerned expression on one side, and a small gray bunny with large worried eyes peeking timidly from behind a bright yellow sunflower on the other side. The background shows the garden in soft, muted colors to emphasize the quiet, careful moment. Both characters look cautious but curious.
illustration.alt: Zara looking concerned while a scared bunny hides behind a sunflower

pages.text: "It's okay, little bunny," Zara whispered in her gentlest voice. She sat very still and slowly held out a small piece of carrot from her picnic basket. The bunny stayed hidden at first, but then took one tiny hop closer. Zara felt so happy when the bunny finally came close enough to eat from her hand. "You're brave," she told her new friend. The bunny's ears perked up, and Zara could tell it was happy too.

illustration.description: Zara sitting cross-legged on the grass with a bright, joyful smile, holding out a small orange carrot piece. The gray bunny is now just inches away, reaching toward the carrot with its front paws. Both look happy and content. Colorful flowers frame the scene, and warm golden sunlight filters through, creating a peaceful, friendship-filled moment with rounded, soft shapes throughout.
illustration.alt: Zara happily feeding a carrot to the now-trusting bunny in the sunny garden

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Zara feel when she first saw the scared bunny?
questions.explanation: Zara felt sad when she saw the frightened little bunny because she cared about how it was feeling.

questions.question: What did Zara give to the bunny?
questions.explanation: Zara gave the bunny a small piece of carrot from her picnic basket to help it feel safe.

questions.question: How did Zara help the scared bunny?
questions.explanation: Zara was gentle and patient, speaking softly and moving slowly so she wouldn't scare the bunny more.

questions.question: How did both Zara and the bunny feel at the end?
questions.explanation: At the end, both Zara and the bunny felt happy because they had made a new friend.

blanks.sentence: Zara loved playing in her backyard ___.
The bunny looked ___ with big worried eyes.
She held out a small piece of ___ from her picnic basket.
Zara felt so ___ when the bunny came close.
